pi_items.source: setup_task.sh Python data generation script dynamically injects sub-99 with a stuck-key response pattern.
setup_task.sh script explicitly models sub-99 by responding 1 (spacebar) on 100% of trials with impossibly fast ~17.5ms RTs, fitting the artifact description.
setup_task.sh loops from 1 to 25 to generate valid human participant records sub-01 through sub-25.
task.json description, setup_task.sh agent instructions, and verifier.py all require and expect this exact output path.

pi_items.source: Data is fully synthetically generated in setup_task.sh via a Python script explicitly stating it matches typical OpenNeuro distributions.

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for pebl_env gng_inhibitory_control_analysis task by confirming the data is generated synthetically via setup_task.sh based on empirical OpenNeuro parameters. Verified exactly 25 real human participants (sub-01 to sub-25) are generated, alongside one intentionally contaminated participant (sub-99) modeling a continuous keypress artifact with ~17.5ms RT and 100% response rate. Verified the exact expected output path '/home/ga/pebl/analysis/gng_report.json' against the task instructions and verifier.py.
